close_reason: Fixed duplicate 'No daemon required', added keyutils(7) reference
description:
secretx-keyring/src/lib.rs:5-19 (module docs): mentions 'persistent keyring' with link to persistent-keyring(7) manpage but does not link to keyutils(7), which is the underlying mechanism the crate actually uses. README.md:18 says 'Requires kernel keyutils support' without linking to keyutils(7) either.

For a contributor or user diagnosing 'keyring not working', the keyutils(7) manpage is the canonical reference for verifying CONFIG_KEYS / CONFIG_PERSISTENT_KEYRINGS, the persistent_keyring_expiry sysctl, and per-user keyring quotas (`/proc/sys/kernel/keys/*`). Linking it directly is worth doing.

Resolution: add 'See keyutils(7) — https://www.man7.org/linux/man-pages/man7/keyutils.7.html — for the kernel API this backend uses' to both module docs and README.

Bus-factor: a future contributor diagnosing weird behavior in restricted environments will not know to look at `/proc/keys` and `/proc/sys/kernel/keys/*` without that pointer.

Note: this finding is interlinked with finding #2 (silent persistent-keyring unavailability). The same docs update should mention the failure mode and the manpage in one place.
title: Module-level docs claim 'No daemon required' twice but don't link to keyutils manpage
